description: Calculate perceived sound frequency using the Doppler effect. Free online calculator to understand how pitch changes with relative motion.
intro: Physical laws are universal and deterministic. The Doppler effect explains how sound frequency changes based on relative motion between source and observer. This calculator applies the standard formula to find the perceived frequency. Fast and free — no signup required. Updated 2026.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **f' = f × (v ± vo) / (v ∓ vs)**. Input your source frequency, velocities, and direction to calculate the perceived frequency shift instantly.
useCases: Technical calculations for Doppler effect applications
Learning and teaching sound physics concepts
explanation:
## How the Doppler Effect Works

The Doppler effect describes how the frequency of sound waves changes based on relative motion between the source and observer. When a source moves toward you, sound waves compress, resulting in higher pitch (higher frequency). When it moves away, waves stretch out, resulting in lower pitch (lower frequency).

## How to Calculate

Enter the source frequency, velocities of the source and observer, the speed of sound in the medium, and whether the source is approaching or receding. The calculator applies the Doppler formula:

**f' = f × (v ± vo) / (v ∓ vs)**

The signs adjust automatically based on your direction selection.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference for educational purposes. For critical applications, consult a professional specialist. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: The Doppler effect is the change in frequency of sound waves caused by relative motion between the source and observer. Approaching sources increase frequency (higher pitch), while receding sources decrease it (lower pitch).

faq.q: Why does an ambulance siren change pitch?
faq.a: As an ambulance approaches, sound waves compress, making the pitch higher. As it passes and moves away, waves stretch, making the pitch lower. This is a real-world example of the Doppler effect in action.

faq.q: How is the Doppler effect used in astronomy?
faq.a: Astronomers use Doppler shift to measure motion of stars and galaxies. Galaxies moving away show redshift (lower frequency), while approaching objects show blueshift. This helps measure cosmic expansion.

faq.q: How do police radar guns work?
faq.a: Police radar emits radio waves at vehicles. Reflected waves return with a different frequency based on the car's speed. Radar calculates this frequency shift using the Doppler effect to determine velocity.
Doppler ultrasound in medicine measures blood flow by detecting frequency shifts of sound waves reflecting off moving blood cells. Higher frequency shifts indicate faster blood flow.
Yes, significantly. Sound travels faster in warmer air. At 20°C, sound travels about 343 m/s. At 0°C, it's only 331 m/s. Adjust your calculation accordingly.
The calculator uses the standard classical Doppler formula, accurate for velocities much less than sound speed. For extreme speeds or shock waves, consult a physics professional.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We don't send data to any server.
